steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The passage claims African languages made "important contributions" to the Puerto Rican dialect, then offers an example using "For example." The blank must complete the phrase "the way certain vowel sounds are pronounced in it can be _____ to how they are pronounced in Yoruba" — meaning the blank must express that the Puerto Rican pronunciations have their origin in Yoruba.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'announced': announced means made known publicly. This creates a nonsensical idiom — one cannot "announce" a pronunciation to another pronunciation — and expresses no linguistic origin. ✗
  • Choice B — 'traced': "traced to" is a standard English idiom meaning attributed to or shown to originate from. "Can be traced to how they are pronounced in Yoruba" directly demonstrates that the Puerto Rican vowel pattern derives from Yoruba, supporting the claim of African linguistic influence. ✓
  • Choice C — 'surrendered': surrendered means given up or yielded. This creates an illogical statement — pronunciations are not surrendered to other pronunciations — and establishes no causal or etymological link. ✗
  • Choice D — 'offered': offered means presented or provided. "Can be offered to how they are pronounced in Yoruba" is grammatically incoherent and expresses no relationship of linguistic origin. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
